question_title: Will Iran's Internet not be "heavily filtered/whitelisted" for most users before May 2026?
summary: Iran's internet has been under whitelist-based heavy filtering since January 8, 2026 (~33 days). The government explicitly stated no restoration before Nowruz (March 20), and is building "Filternet Plus" national intranet infrastructure for prolonged shutdowns. The crackdown is the worst since 1979 (3,000-6,000+ killed), giving the regime strong incentive to maintain information control. While economic costs of whitelist-based filtering are enormous and create pressure for eventual restoration, the combination of stated government intent, unprecedented crackdown severity, new infrastructure capabilities, and the specific resolution requirement (NetBlocks must report NOT heavily filtered for 24+ hours) make it more likely than not that heavy filtering persists through May 1. The ~40-day window between Nowruz and the deadline provides some opportunity for restoration, but transition would likely be gradual and may still be characterized as "heavily filtered."

factors.description: Government explicitly stated no internet restoration before Nowruz (March 20, 2026), plus extension past 40-day mourning period
Iran building 'Filternet Plus' national intranet and 'internet kill switch' infrastructure designed for prolonged shutdowns - structural shift not just temporary measure
Unprecedented crackdown scale (3,000-6,000+ killed, 42,000+ arrested) creates extremely strong regime incentive to maintain information blackout during ongoing arrests
Shutdown already at 33+ days, far exceeding 2019 precedent (~1 week). Current situation is qualitatively different from past shutdowns.
Economic costs of whitelist-based filtering are enormous and mounting - Iran's economy was already in crisis from currency collapse. Pressure for eventual restoration is real.
80 days remain until deadline; even with government's minimum timeline (Nowruz), ~40 days window exists for potential restoration before May 1
Iran has always eventually restored internet after past shutdowns - total permanent whitelist is historically unprecedented
Resolution requires specific NetBlocks reporting of NOT heavily filtered for 24+ hours - even partial restoration may not meet this bar during gradual transition
